name: Sierra de la Cruz Hermitage Loop
name_original: De Piedrabuena a la Sierra de la Cruz — circular por la Ermita de la Sierra de la Cruz
description: This difficult 6.6-mile jogging route from Piedrabuena to Sierra de la Cruz offers challenging ascents and potential panoramic views.
region_intro: Jogging routes around Piedrabuena offer a variety of terrain for runners. The region features paths along natural waterways like the Río Bullaque and Río Guadiana, as well as routes leading to higher ground such as Sierra de la Cruz. The landscape includes rolling hills and agricultural areas, providing diverse running experiences. Best jogging routes around Piedrabuena The most popular jogging route is Sierra de la Cruz Hermitage Loop, a 6.6 miles (10.7 km) trail that takes 1 hour 24 minutes to complete. This route features significant ascents and offers panoramic views. Another top favourite among local runners is Alcolea Trail loop from Alcolea de Calatrava, a difficult 13.3 miles (21.4 km) path. This long-distance route traverses varied terrain with substantial elevation gain. Local runners also love the Tabla de la Yedra loop from Piedrabuena, a 9.8 miles (15.7 km) trail leading through the area of Tabla de la Yedra, often completed in about 1 hour 38 minutes. Jogging around Piedrabuena is defined by river paths, rolling hills, and agricultural landscapes. The network offers options for moderate and challenging ability levels. The routes in Piedrabuena are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 2 reviews. More than 50 runners have used komoot to explore Piedrabuena's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Piedrabuena?
A: There are over 8 dedicated running routes around Piedrabuena, offering a variety of experiences for different fitness levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the jogging routes in Piedrabuena?
A: The jogging routes in Piedrabuena feature diverse terrain, including paths along natural waterways like the Río Bullaque and Río Guadiana, rolling hills, and agricultural landscapes. Some routes also lead to higher ground, such as the Sierra de la Cruz, offering significant ascents.

Q: Are there challenging running trails in Piedrabuena for experienced runners?
A: Yes, Piedrabuena offers several challenging routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19411022" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcolea Trail loop from Alcolea de Calatrava&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 13.3-mile path with substantial elevation gain. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1211406578/sierra-de-la-cruz-hermitage-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de la Cruz Hermitage Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which includes significant ascents and panoramic views.

Q: What are some moderate running options in Piedrabuena?
A: For a moderate challenge, you can explore routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10348247" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Alcolea de Calatrava&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 7.2 miles, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10348238" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Piedrabuena&lt;/a&gt;, a 6.3-mile path with gentler elevation changes.

Q: Can I find circular running routes in Piedrabuena?
A: Absolutely. Many of the running routes in Piedrabuena are circular, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1211406578/sierra-de-la-cruz-hermitage-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de la Cruz Hermitage Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40013494" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tabla de la Yedra loop from Piedrabuena&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while running in Piedrabuena?
A: While running, you can encounter various natural and man-made landmarks. The region features routes along the Río Bullaque and Río Guadiana. You might also pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5896798" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vicario Dam&lt;/a&gt;, the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1064593" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sheep Bridge&lt;/a&gt;, or areas like Tabla de la Yedra. The Sierra de la Cruz offers panoramic views.

Q: Are there any historical sites accessible via running trails?
A: Yes, some trails offer glimpses of historical interest. The Sierra de la Cruz route leads towards a hermitage, and the broader region includes sites like Mortara Castle, which can be part of longer hiking or running explorations.

Q: Are the running routes in Piedrabuena suitable for families?
A: While specific family-friendly routes aren't explicitly highlighted, the region offers easy hikes along the Bullaque River that are suitable for all fitness levels. For running, moderate routes with less elevation gain, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10348238" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Piedrabuena&lt;/a&gt;, could be suitable for active families.

Q: Are there dog-friendly running trails in Piedrabuena?
A: Many outdoor trails in Piedrabuena are generally suitable for dogs, provided they are kept on a leash and owners adhere to local regulations regarding pet waste. It's always a good idea to check specific trail rules or local signage before heading out with your dog.

Q: What do other runners enjoy the most about jogging in Piedrabuena?
A: The running routes in Piedrabuena are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Runners often praise the varied terrain, from river paths to rolling hills, and the diverse running experiences available.

Q: What is the best time of year to go jogging in Piedrabuena?
A: Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions for jogging in Piedrabuena, with milder temperatures. Summers can be quite hot, so early morning or late evening runs are advisable. Winters are usually mild, making year-round running possible, though some days might be cooler.
tips:
It is located to the southwest of the town, nestled at the top of the mountain range that gives it its name, 3km from Piedrabuena and 791m high.

From this viewpoint you can see the town and surroundings, such as the Piedrabuena volcano. An important monument that we find in the place is the Hermitage Sierra de la Cruz, built at the end of the 19th century.

Every May 3rd, the Piedrabuena pilgrimage is celebrated on this hill.
Located in the Sierra de la Cruz not far from the Ermita de la Sierra de la Cruz in Piedrabuena, it is worth the climb for the excellent views.

Located in the Maiyor square next to the Town Hall. It is a Neoclassical style construction. Among the construction materials, brick predominates, and of course volcanic stone, and the metallic roofs of the towers stand out. Very nice in general everything.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this jogging route?
faqs.answer: This jogging route is approximately 10.7 km (6.6 miles) long. On average, it takes about 1 hour and 24 minutes to complete, though this can vary based on your fitness level and pace.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this trail is rated as difficult and is considered a 'Hard run' requiring a 'Very good fitness' level. It features significant elevation changes, including a gain of 330 meters (1082 feet), making it challenging for those new to jogging or trail running.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is primarily composed of mostly paved surfaces. However, despite the paved nature, the 'Hard run' rating indicates there are likely steep sections that contribute to its difficulty.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: While specific parking details for the exact trailhead are not provided, users often look for parking near the Hermitage of the Sierra de la Cruz (Piedrabuena), which is a key highlight on this circular route.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the trail?
faqs.answer: Information regarding dog-friendliness and specific leash rules for this particular trail is not explicitly available. It's always best to check local regulations or signage at the trailhead before bringing your dog.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay an entrance fee for this route?
faqs.answer: There is no information indicating that permits or entrance fees are required to access this jogging route. It appears to be freely accessible.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to jog this route would generally be during milder seasons like spring or autumn to avoid the extreme heat of summer or potential colder, wetter conditions in winter. Always check the local weather forecast before heading out.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: Key highlights along this circular route include the Hermitage of the Sierra de la Cruz (Piedrabuena), the Piedrabuena Antennas, and the Our Lady of the Assumption (Piedrabuena). The hermitage, in particular, is likely to offer elevated vistas.

faqs.question: Are there good viewpoints on the trail?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's name, 'Sierra de la Cruz,' and the presence of the Hermitage of the Sierra de la Cruz, it is highly probable that there are sections offering panoramic views and elevated vistas, especially from the higher points of the route.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or pubs near the trailhead in Piedrabuena?
faqs.answer: Piedrabuena is a town, so you can expect to find local amenities like cafes and pubs there. It's a good idea to check local listings for specific establishments that might be convenient after your run.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, a small portion of this route, approximately 4%, follows the official path known as Calle Ciudad Real.
